data.title: NLS Pharmaceutics files to sell 5M common shares for holders
data.description:
• NLS Pharmaceutics (NASDAQ:NLSP) has filed to sell 5M common shares for holders.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Megan announces pricing of $5 million initial public offering
data.description:
• Megan (NASDAQ:MGN) announced on Thursday the pricing of its initial public offering of 1.25 million ordinary shares at $4.00 per ordinary share.
  •  The company has granted a 45-day option to the underwriters to purchase up to 187,500 additional ordinary shares solely to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The ordinary shares have been approved for listing on Nasdaq and are expected to commence trading on September 26, 2025, under the ticker symbol “MGN.”
  • The offering is expected to close on or about September 29, 2025.
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $5 million from the offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses.
  • Proceeds from the offering will be used for sales and marketing, expansion opportunities through merger and acquisition activities, development of our “Smart Farming System” and general working capital and corporate purposes, said the company. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PSEG Long Island wins grid deal extension through 2030 despite lawsuit
data.description:
Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) said Thursday that the The Long Island Power Authority awarded its PSEG Long Island subsidiary a five-year extension of the existing contract to operate the electric grid on Long Island and in the Rockaways.

LIPA said the agreement, which begins January 1, 2026, reduces costs, tightens budget controls, increases PSEG Long Island's liability cap for certain events, and enhances LIPA's oversight of storm preparedness and technical standards, while claiming the new deal saves ratepayers an estimated $17 million in management fees over the existing contract.

The LIPA board earlier this year rejected a bid to replace PSEG Long Island with Quanta Services (PWR) to run Long Island's electric grid; Quanta recently filed a lawsuit seeking to block LIPA's current negotiations and keep LIPA from extending the PSEG Long Island contract.

The contract extension requires approval by the New York State Comptroller and the New York State Attorney General.

data.title: Costco beats on top- and bottom-line but comparable stores sales slow
data.description:
Consumers continued to flock to Costco (NASDAQ:COST), taking advantage of the warehouse store’s sizable bulk-rate savings fourth quarter results beating Wall Street’s expectations on nearly every metric, including profit and sales.

Shares were under pressure in after hours trading, however, as comparable sales in the U.S. were slightly below expectations and overall company comp sales decelerated from the previous quarter, an unexpected development given the lingering inflationary and tariff environment that favors value positioning among consumers

For the fiscal fourth quarter ending August 31, Costco (NASDAQ:COST) earned a profit of $5.87 per share, up 11% from a year ago and 7 cents better than expected.

With $1.72B contributed by membership fees – up 14% from a year ago and almost 2% better than expected -- total sales increased 8.1% to $86.16B and beat $86.06B estimates.

Comparable store sales excluding gas sales in the U.S. were up 6.0%, down from +7.9% in fiscal Q3 and missing +6.05% estimates. In Canada, adjusted comparable store sales rose 8.3%, up from 7.8% last quarter and better than +6.83% estimates. Company-wide, comparable store sales excluding fuel sales rose 6.4% versus 8.0% previously but still above 6.21% estimates. Finally, e-commerce store sales increased 13.5% versus 15.7%, previously.

At the end of the quarter, Costco (NASDAQ:COST) operated 914 warehouse stores, 9 more than last quarter including 629 in the United States and Puerto Rico (+5), 110 in Canada (+1), 42 in Mexico (+1), 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 20 in Korea (+1), 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, seven in China, five in Spain, two in France, two in Sweden, and one each in Iceland, and New Zealand. Costco also operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Mexico, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia.

data.title: Interfor announces $125M bought deal offering of common shares.
data.description:
• Interfor (TSX:IFP:CA)on Thursday announced a bought deal offering of 12.43M common shares of the company at a price of $10.05 per common share for gross proceeds of $125 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of the offering to reduce debt and for general corporate purposes.
  • Proceeds of the offering are expected to further enhance the company's flexibility to navigate near-term market volatility, it said.
  • The offering is scheduled to close on or about October 1, 2025.

data.title: Concentrix Non-GAAP EPS of $2.78 misses by $0.09, revenue of $2.48B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Concentrix press release (NASDAQ:CNXC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.78 misses by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $2.48B (+4.2% Y/Y) beats by $20M.

data.title: CNH Industrial Capital prices $500M of 4.500% notes due 2030
data.description:
• CNH Industrial Capital, a subsidiary of CNH Industrial N.V. (NYSE:CNH), has priced $500M of 4.500% senior unsecured notes due 2030.
  • The notes were priced at 99.765% of par value.
  • Closing is expected on September 29, 2025.
  • Interest will be paid semi-annually on April 16 and October 16, starting April 16, 2026.
  • Notes will mature on October 16, 2030.
  • The offering is guaranteed by CNH Industrial Capital America and New Holland Credit Company.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including working capital, asset purchases, and debt repayment.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Enact Mortgage Insurance enters into quota share reinsurance agreement
data.description:
• Enact (NASDAQ:ACT) announced on Thursday that its flagship legal entity, Enact Mortgage Insurance, has entered into a quota share reinsurance agreement with a broad panel of reinsurers each currently rated “A-” or better by Standard & Poor’s (“S&P”) or A.M. Best Company, Inc., or rated “A3” or better by Moody’s.
  • Under the agreement, and subject to certain conditions, Enact will cede approximately 34% of a portion of expected new insurance written for the period from January 1, 2027, through December 31, 2027.
  • “This new quota share agreement underscores our commitment to disciplined risk management and efficient capital deployment,” said Rohit Gupta, President and CEO of Enact. “The transaction supports our pursuit of high-quality new business and improves the resilience of our portfolio. We are grateful for the partnership of our reinsurers and remain focused on advancing our mission and driving long-term value creation.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stifel client assets rise 2% in August, buoyed by recruiting, market gains
data.description:
Stifel Financiual Corp.'s (NYSE:SF) total client assets were at a record $532.7B at the end of August, up 2% from a month earlier and 9% Y/Y, driven by robust recruiting and market appreciation, it said on Thursday.

Fee-based client assets also hit a record $213.6B last month, up 2% from July and 14% from the year-ago period.

Private Client Group fee-based client assets of $186.7B compared with $182.5B in July and $163.8B a year before.

Momentum is building in our Institutional Group as the capital raising and M&A environment has improved significantly since earlier this year," said Stifel Chairman and CEO Ronald J. Kruszewski. "Barring a significant market downturn, we anticipate a stronger second half of the year for this segment.”

Bank loans, net (includes loans held for sale), were $21.7B in August, virtually unchanged from the previous month and up from $20.3B in August 2024.

Client money market and insured product slid 1% M/M and 4% Y/Y to $25.4B.

data.title: Intel surges as investment news bubbles; PC market stronger than expected
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares surged 8.8% by the close of market trading on Thursday, as the once-beleaguered U.S. chipmaker has climbed 37% since September 17.

Its share price has soared 69% year to date. In comparison, the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) has increased 26% year to date, while the S&P 500 Index is up 12% over the same time frame. 

Interestingly, most major semiconductor stocks finished the trading day down or remained mostly static. For example, TSMC (TSM) slid 1.5%, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) inched up 0.1%, Nvidia (NVDA) increased 0.3%, Broadcom (AVGO) slipped 0.9% and Qualcomm (QCOM) declined 2.2%. However, Marvell (MRVL) gained 4.6%.

Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has recently secured significant investments in his company. This includes a $2B investment by Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF), a 10% stake by the U.S. government and, most recently, GPU giant Nvidia's $5B backing.

Rumors surfaced on Wednesday that Intel was in discussions with Apple (AAPL) for a potential investment or a possible partnership. It has also been speculated that Intel and TSMC have reached a preliminary agreement to form a joint venture to operate some of Intel's foundry facilities. This report first surfaced way back in April, but was echoed again today in the Wall Street Journal. 

There has also been positive news recently on a strengthening PC market. The PC segment stands as Intel's most substantial driver of revenue. Micron Technology indicated earlier this week that the PC market is improving due to the Windows 11 upgrade and the emergence of artificial intelligence-powered PCs.

"Turning to PCs, end-of-life of Windows 10 and greater adoption of AI-enabled PCs are driving an improved PC demand outlook," said Micron CEO during Tuesday night's earnings call. "We now expect PC unit shipments to grow at a mid-single-digit percentage level in calendar 2025 versus our low single-digit percentage growth expectations previously."
